summary: The candidate CM, its cnos schema projection, validator, recursive manifests, and fail-closed historical preflight describe one stable methodology package. It remains a candidate rather than a deployed instrument because current TSC cannot consume its arbitrary methodology declaration first-class and its calibration, held-out, and consistency program has no standing yet.

positive: SKILL.md applies one L0-L4 recursion and one alpha/beta/gamma vocabulary across cell classes, kernels, typed surfaces, FSMs, and executions.
The classifier, CM-to-V-to-delta boundary, thirteen hard invariants, and disposition rules are stable across the authority, instruction, README, and calibration record.
schemas/coherence_methodology.cue types the methodology essence as a pinned projection of the named TSC revision.
alpha.reason: The bundle has a stable local pattern language and no detected internal definitional contradiction.

positive: The cnos skill validator recognizes artifact_class measurement, vets the generic methodology block, resolves local authority paths, and verifies every declared target is registered.
The registry resolves six manifests whose L0-L4 contents match the methodology's recursion.
verify-target.sh binds the historical calibration to receipt head a0d39293, matter revision 2d6b93cc, and exact matter SHA-256, failing on other checkouts.
README.md and the calibration record distinguish the candidate CM, historical corpus, and current repository state.
negative: The present TSC CLI executes the manifests only through its built-in instruction; it cannot consume this methodology declaration and INSTRUCTION.md as one arbitrary pinned instrument.
beta.reason: All in-repository authority and calibration edges are materialized; the remaining systemic relation gap is between the declared third-party CM and the generic TSC executor.

positive: The status section forbids self-warrant and lists explicit prerequisites for standing.
The historical target preflight prevents a later #662 revision from silently replacing R7 calibration bytes.
The calibration commons section names positive, adversarial, comparative, and held-out cases required for promotion.
negative: No owned cycle or durable transition is named for producing the public calibration commons, three-sample semantic consistency, and held-out anchor.
No versioned executor migration connects the essence-only declaration to future arbitrary-CM execution.
This self-measure is one same-author semantic sample, so it confers no independent or consistency standing.
gamma.reason: The methodology is evolution-honest and fail-closed, but its promotion and executor paths are specified rather than owned or shipped.

defect_cards.evidence: SKILL.md status boundary; README.md application procedure
defect_cards.summary: Current TSC cannot execute the declaration, target projection, and semantic instruction as one arbitrary pinned CM.

defect_cards.evidence: SKILL.md status prerequisites and calibration commons
defect_cards.summary: Independent calibration, consistency sampling, and held-out validation have requirements but no owned cycle.
No versioned migration turns the essence-only candidate into a deployed arbitrary-CM instrument.

next_fixes.fix: Implement and version TSC arbitrary-CM loading so the declaration, projection, instruction, and report provenance become one executable edge.
Open owned calibration and held-out cycles, obtain three frozen-prompt semantic samples, and measure consistency before granting standing.
